dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9443 IH: Saving FACE Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Ogles (for himself, Mr. Smith of New Jersey, Mrs. Biggs of South Carolina, Mr. Stutzman, and Mr. Cloud) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend title 18, United States Code, to remove criminal penalties for obstructing access to abortion services, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Saving FACE Act of 2026.

section.header: Removing criminal penalties for obstructing access to abortion services

subsection.text: Section 248 of title 18, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in the section heading, by striking clinic and inserting church;
in subsection (d)(4), by striking abortions or other; and

subparagraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (1) through (6) as paragraphs (2) through (7), respectively;
by inserting before paragraph (2), as so redesignated, the following:

paragraph.text: The term abortion means the use or prescription of any instrument, drug, substance, or device—

subparagraph.text: to intentionally kill the unborn child of a woman known to be pregnant; or
to intentionally terminate the pregnancy of a woman known to be pregnant, with an intention other than—

clause.text: after viability, to produce a live birth and preserve the life and health of the child born alive; or
to remove a dead unborn child.

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (6), as so redesignated, by striking relating to the human reproductive system and all that follows through the period at the end and inserting relating to the human reproductive system or pregnancy, excluding abortions..

subsection.text: The table of sections for chapter 13 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by striking the item related to section 248 and inserting the following:

toc.toc-entry: 248. Freedom of access to church entrances.
